prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh sweetie, you know how our whole house runs because of your grandpa's brain telling everyone what to do and when? Well, a CPU is sort of like the brain of the computer! It's a tiny little chip tucked inside the machine that thinks through all the instructions and figures out what the computer should do next. Every time you click something or type a letter, that little brain is working away, quick as a wink, making things happen on your screen. I don't fully understand all the ins and outs of it myself, but I do know that without it, the whole computer would just sit there like a bump on a log! Isn't it something, how they fit all that thinking into such a tiny little thing?
